questions.question: In the document hierarchy governing a VT job, which statement is correct?
options: A specification is a national consensus document, while codes are contract documents
A code is a body of rules suitable for adoption into law, while a specification states contract-specific requirements such as the governing edition
An employer procedure outranks the code when the two conflict
questions.explanation: Codes are comprehensive rule sets that jurisdictions can adopt into law; specifications carry the contract-level choices — which code, which edition, and any customer additions — and requirements only flow down the stack.

questions.question: Which document is largely self-contained, providing both the examination method and the visual acceptance criteria for structural steel welds?
questions.explanation: AWS D1.1 carries its own visual acceptance table, so method and acceptance live in one book; ASME Section V Article 9 is method-only, and the other two are personnel documents.

questions.question: Within the European system, EN 13445 for unfired pressure vessels functions as:
options: EU law that replaces the Pressure Equipment Directive
A harmonized standard giving a presumption of conformity with the PED's essential safety requirements
questions.explanation: The PED is the law and states essential safety requirements; harmonized standards such as EN 13445 provide a presumption of conformity when equipment is designed and inspected to them.

questions.question: Under the PED, NDT of permanent joints on category III and IV pressure equipment must be performed by personnel who are:
options: Approved by a recognized third-party organization
Certified internally by the fabricator's human resources department
Graduates of any visual testing course
questions.explanation: For the higher hazard categories, the PED requires NDT personnel for permanent joints to be approved by a recognized third-party organization — the central-certification model reflected in EN ISO 9712.

questions.question: Your employer's procedure states a minimum of 100 footcandles for critical examination. That equals approximately:
questions.explanation: One footcandle is about 10.76 lux, so 100 fc is roughly 1076 lux — the familiar 1000 lux critical-examination minimum.

questions.question: Glare from a polished stainless surface is washing out your view of a weld toe. The best fix is to:
options: Diffuse the light source or move your eye or the lamp off the specular angle
Examine from farther than 600 mm away
questions.explanation: Glare is specular reflection bouncing straight into your eye — a geometry problem solved by diffusing the source or changing the angle, not by adding brightness, which makes it worse.

questions.question: Straw, blue, or grey discoloration along a titanium weld bead most likely indicates:
options: Normal coloration that can be ignored
Atmospheric contamination of the hot weld metal, to be evaluated against the governing criteria
Paint residue from the layout crew
questions.explanation: Titanium discolors when the hot metal is exposed to air; the color sequence signals increasing contamination, and the governing specification dictates what is acceptable.

questions.question: During in-process VT near active welding, the correct eye-protection practice is to:
options: Watch the arc briefly, since short exposures are harmless
Never view the arc without appropriate filtered protection, and shield against UV and spatter
Close one eye while observing the arc
questions.explanation: Arc UV causes flash burn even at short exposures and from reflections; in-process examination positions you close to the arc, so filtered protection and shielding are mandatory.

questions.question: Comparing a relevant indication against the documented acceptance criteria is, by definition:
questions.explanation: Interpretation decides what an indication is; evaluation is the separate step of comparing a relevant indication to the acceptance criteria to accept or reject.

questions.question: An employer limits VT certification to direct visual inspection only. Which topic must still be trained?
options: Lighting verification and direct viewing geometry
questions.explanation: Only topics that exist solely for remote or enhanced techniques may be deleted; lighting, viewing geometry, and the rest of the direct-VT core always remain in the outline.
